Medical Nutrition Therapist
Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT) Guide

Issued by the Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure under the Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS). Data verified for 2026.

Total Initial Fee
$114
$114 initial application fee
Renewal
$114
Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Fax: (402) 742-1106

Credential Categories

Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist License Types

Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT)

In Nebraska, dietitians are licensed as LMNTs. An LMNT license is required specifically to provide medical nutrition therapy (MNT), which involves treating diseases or medical conditions.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Nebraska Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD or RDN status satisfies this and the education requirements.

Submit the Application to DHHS

Complete the application form for Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure and submit it with the $114 fee, proof of age (minimum 19), and proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.

Provide Verification & Fingerprints

Send a copy of your active CDR card or have primary source verification sent. Starting January 1, 2026, all DHHS applicants must undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration VerificationVerification of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Proof of Age & Legal PresenceA copy of a birth certificate, driver's license, passport, or similar documentation.
  • Fingerprint Background checkRequired for all new applications starting in 2026.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Nebraska does not specify a provisional LMNT license option. An applicant must meet all qualifications including examination prior to licensure.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Licensure by reciprocity is available for an applicant currently licensed in another state. The requirements of that state must be substantially equivalent to Nebraska's. Verification of all held licenses and active CDR registration is required, along with the $114 application fee.

Frequently Asked Questions

Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist FAQ

How much does a Nebraska LMNT license cost?

The initial license application fee is $114.

When does the Nebraska license need to be renewed?

Nebraska LMNT licenses expire on September 1 of all odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $114.

Is the title 'Dietitian' licensed in Nebraska?

No, Nebraska licenses the specific practice of Medical Nutrition Therapy, granting the title Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT). General nutrition services may not require a license, but only an LMNT can provide medical nutrition therapy.
